topics: Hrs. 30 Min. Prerequisites (if any) None Course Type Lab Course Objectives
1. To familiarize students with various material properties such as elasticity
yield strength
modulus of elasticity

etc. 2. To enable students to analyze and evaluate the behavior of structural components under different loading conditions. 3. To bridge the gap between theoretical knowledge gained in lectures and its practical application in real-world scenarios. Expt. No. Experiments 1 Determination of hardness of the given specimen by Brinell’s Hardness test. 2 Determination of hardness of the given specimen by Rockwell Hardness test. 3 Determination of impact load on a specimen by Izod impact Test. 4 Determination of impact load on a specimen by Charpy’s impact test. 5 Determination of modulus of elasticity
ultimate stress
permissible stress

percentage elongation and fracture properties of M. S. and TMT specimens by conducting Tension test in Universal Testing Machine. 6 Determination of compressive strength of concrete cylinders and concrete cubes using compression testing machine. *Minimum of 10 experiment Course Assessment Method (CIE

Viva and Timely completion of Lab Reports / Record (Continuous Assessment) Internal Examination Total 5 25 20 50 7 Determination of the various physical and mechanical properties of M.S. Girder
Timber beam

R.C.C. beam etc by performing the bending test in Universal Testing Machine. 8 Determination of ultimate shear strength of mild steel by double shear test. 9 Determine modulus of elasticity of beam using Maxwell’s reciprocal theorem. 10 Determination of Modulus of Rigidity of mild steel specimen by performing the torsion test. 11 Analysis of the load deflection characteristics of close coiled spring (tension spring) using spring testing machine. 12 Analysis of the load deflection characteristics of open coiled spring (compression spring) using spring testing machine. 13 Determination of workability of concrete by slump test. 14 Determination of workability of concrete by compaction factor test. 15 Determination of initial and final setting time for cement. 16 Determination of the fatigue behaviour of a specimen by using Fatigue test machine. Course Outcome Bloom’s Knowledge Level (KL) CO1 Gain familiarity with the operation and calibration of laboratory equipment used for material testing
such as universal testing machines
extensometers
and hardness testers. K2 CO2 Develop the ability to analyze experimental data obtained from material testing
including stress-strain curves
load-displacement curves
and failure modes
and draw meaningful conclusions from the results. K4 CO3 Acquire hands-on experience in determining mechanical properties of materials through experimentation
including tensile strength
yield strength
modulus of elasticity
workability and hardness. K3 CO4 Understand the relationship between experimental observations and theoretical concepts learned in the classroom
and recognize the limitations and assumptions of theoretical models compared to real-world behavior. K2 CO5 Demonstrate a commitment to laboratory safety protocols and practices
including proper handling of equipment
use of personal protective equipment (PPE
and adherence to standard operating procedures. K3 Note

Preparation and Pre-Lab Work (7 Marks)  Pre-Lab Assignments
Assessment of pre-lab assignments or quizzes that test understanding of the upcoming experiment.  Understanding of Theory
Evaluation based on students’ preparation and understanding of the theoretical background related to the experiments. 2. Conduct of Experiments (7 Marks)  Procedure and Execution
Adherence to correct procedures
accurate execution of experiments
and following safety protocols.  Skill Proficiency
Proficiency in handling equipment
accuracy in observations
and troubleshooting skills during the experiments.  Teamwork
Collaboration and participation in group experiments. 3. Lab Reports and Record Keeping (6 Marks)  Quality of Reports
Clarity
completeness and accuracy of lab reports. Proper documentation of experiments
data analysis and conclusions.  Timely Submission
Adhering to deadlines for submitting lab reports/rough record and maintaining a well-organized fair record. 4. Viva Voce (5 Marks)  Oral Examination
Ability to explain the experiment
results and underlying principles during a viva voce session. Final Marks Averaging
The final marks for preparation
conduct of experiments
viva

and record are the average of all the specified experiments in the syllabus. Evaluation Pattern for End Semester Examination (50 Marks) 1. Procedure/Preliminary Work/Design/Algorithm (10 Marks)  Procedure Understanding and Description
Clarity in explaining the procedure and understanding each step involved.  Preliminary Work and Planning
Thoroughness in planning and organizing materials/equipment.  Algorithm Development

Correctness and efficiency of the algorithm related to the experiment.  Creativity and logic in algorithm or experimental design. 2. Conduct of Experiment/Execution of Work/Programming (15 Marks)  Setup and Execution
Proper setup and accurate execution of the experiment or programming task. 3. Result with Valid Inference/Quality of Output (10 Marks)  Accuracy of Results
Precision and correctness of the obtained results.  Analysis and Interpretation
Validity of inferences drawn from the experiment or quality of program output. 4. Viva Voce (10 Marks)  Ability to explain the experiment
procedure results and answer related questions  Proficiency in answering questions related to theoretical and practical aspects of the subject. 5. Record (5 Marks)  Completeness
clarity
and accuracy of the lab record submitted SEMESTER 4 AGRICULTURE ENGINEERING SEMESTER S4 MATHEMATICS FOR PHYSICAL SCIENCE - 4 (Group C
